overview: Build all Express API endpoints on top of the foundation (scaffold + SQLite + ingest already complete). Covers search/filter, asset detail, filter options, file upload, and OpenAI Vision enrichment. Run this PRD in a feature/api worktree in parallel with the Frontend PRD after the Foundation PRD has been merged.
goals: Expose a full-text natural language search endpoint with structured filter support
Expose asset detail endpoint with quality issue analysis and CDN URL
Expose filter options endpoint for dynamic UI population
Accept image file uploads (single or multiple) and persist to disk + SQLite
Enrich uploaded assets with OpenAI GPT-4o Vision — title, description, tags, location, mood, scene
nonGoals: No React UI work — that is the Frontend PRD
No write-back to AEM or .content.xml files
successMetrics: POST /api/assets/upload accepts a JPEG and returns a new asset record
POST /api/assets/:id/enrich calls OpenAI Vision and writes enriched fields to SQLite
All quality gates pass: npm run lint, npm run build, npm test
openQuestions: Use gpt-4o or gpt-4o-mini for enrichment — gpt-4o for quality, gpt-4o-mini for cost?
assumptions: Foundation PRD is complete and merged — scaffold, SQLite schema, FTS5 triggers, and 49 ingested assets all exist
server/ directory structure, package.json scripts, and db/index.js are already in place
uploads/ directory exists at project root

stack.ai: OpenAI GPT-4o Vision API via openai npm package (already installed)

routes.name: Search Assets
routes.purpose: Full-text + structured filter search. Params: q, category, subcategory, rights_status, location, has_title, has_rights, has_release_placeholder, limit, offset

routes.name: Asset Detail
routes.purpose: Full asset record with quality_issues array and cdn_url

routes.name: Filter Options
routes.purpose: Distinct filter values + metadata counts for UI population

routes.name: Upload Asset(s)
routes.purpose: Multipart file upload — saves to disk, inserts to DB, triggers enrichment

routes.name: Enrich Asset
routes.purpose: OpenAI Vision enrichment — updates enriched_* fields and FTS index

rules: display_title: enriched_title ?? original_title ?? filename without extension
display_tags: merge + deduplicate enriched_tags and original_tags arrays
cdn_url: scene7_domain.trimEnd('/') + '/is/image/' + scene7_file — null if either field is missing
FTS search: use assets_fts MATCH query with the q param; fall back to LIKE %q% on key columns if FTS returns zero results
Upload file storage: uploads/<uuid>-<sanitized-originalname> relative to project root
Upload MIME whitelist: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp
Upload size limit: 20MB per file (multer limits.fileSize)
Enrichment prompt instructs GPT-4o to respond ONLY with valid JSON: { title, description, tags, location, scene, subjects, mood, channel_hint, confidence }
After enrichment UPDATE, FTS trigger fires automatically — no manual FTS sync needed
All routes mounted under /api — register in server/src/index.js

stories.title: GET /api/assets — search and filter endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want to search and filter assets via the API so I can find relevant images for my campaign.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/routes/assets.js created — exports an Express Router
server/src/services/search.js exports searchAssets(db, params) function
GET /api/assets with no params returns 200 { total: 49, assets: [...] }
Query param q: runs SELECT from assets JOIN assets_fts WHERE assets_fts MATCH ? — returns ranked results by FTS rank
Query param category: adds WHERE category = ? to query
Query param subcategory: adds WHERE subcategory = ? to query
Query param rights_status: filters on enriched_rights_status column (values: owned, unclear, none)
Query param location: filters on enriched_location or original_location
Query param has_title=true: WHERE (original_title IS NOT NULL AND original_title != '') OR (enriched_title IS NOT NULL AND enriched_title != '')
Query param has_rights=true: WHERE enriched_rights_status != 'none'
Query param has_release_placeholder=true: WHERE has_release_placeholder = 1
Query params limit (default 100, max 200) and offset (default 0) for pagination

Each asset in response includes: id, filename, category, subcategory, display_title, display_description, display_tags, thumbnail_path, web_image_path, enriched_rights_status, enrichment_source, width, height, scene7_file, scene7_domain, s7_sync_error, needs_rights_review, has_release_placeholder
Route registered in server/src/index.js: app.use('/api/assets', assetsRouter)
Supertest test: GET /api/assets returns 200 with total 49
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?q=sunset returns at least 1 result
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?category=ships returns only assets with category='ships'
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?rights_status=none returns exactly 27 assets
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?limit=5&offset=0 returns assets.length=5 and total=49
Negative case: GET /api/assets?q=zzznomatch returns 200 { total: 0, assets: [] }

stories.title: GET /api/assets/:id — asset detail endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want full metadata for a single asset including quality analysis and CDN URL so I can review and use the asset.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/assets/:id returns 200 with the full assets row plus computed fields
Response includes cdn_url computed from scene7_domain + '/is/image/' + scene7_file (null if either missing)

Response includes quality_issues: array of strings for each issue present — 'missing_title' if both original_title and enriched_title are null/empty; 'missing_description' if both are null/empty; 'missing_rights' if enriched_rights_status = 'none'; 'missing_location' if both location fields are null/empty; 'title_equals_description' if original_title == original_description (non-null); 's7_sync_error' if s7_sync_error = 1; 'release_placeholder' if has_release_placeholder = 1
Response includes display_title, display_description, display_tags computed fields
Supertest test: GET /api/assets/anthem-of-the-seas-new-york-statue-liberty.jpg returns correct original_title
Supertest test: an asset with enriched_rights_status='none' has 'missing_rights' in quality_issues
Supertest test: an asset with has_release_placeholder=1 has 'release_placeholder' in quality_issues
Supertest test: cdn_url is a correctly formatted string for an asset with scene7_file set
Negative case: GET /api/assets/does-not-exist returns 404 { error: 'Asset not found' }

stories.title: GET /api/filters — filter options endpoint
stories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a filters endpoint that returns all distinct filter values and counts so the UI can populate filters dynamically.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/routes/filters.js created — exports an Express Router
GET /api/filters returns 200 with { categories, subcategories, locations, rights_statuses, counts }
categories: sorted array of distinct non-null category values
subcategories: object keyed by category, each value is sorted array of subcategory strings e.g. { ships: ['allure','anthem','grandeur'], promotions: ['asia','casino','star-and-icon'] }
locations: sorted array of distinct non-null values from both original_location and enriched_location, merged and deduplicated
rights_statuses: array of { value, count } objects for 'owned', 'unclear', 'none'
counts: { total, missing_title, missing_description, missing_rights, missing_location, has_release_placeholder, s7_sync_error } — each is an integer
Route registered in server/src/index.js: app.use('/api/filters', filtersRouter)
Supertest test: categories contains 'ships' and 'promotions'
Supertest test: subcategories.ships contains 'allure', 'anthem', 'grandeur'
Supertest test: counts.has_release_placeholder equals 7
Supertest test: counts.total equals 49
Negative case: empty database returns all arrays empty and all counts 0

stories.title: POST /api/assets/upload — multi-file upload endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want to upload image files so they are saved to disk and registered in the database ready for enrichment.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/routes/upload.js created — exports an Express Router
POST /api/assets/upload accepts multipart/form-data with field name 'files' using multer().array('files')

multer configured with: storage = diskStorage saving to uploads/ with filename <uuid>-<originalname>, fileFilter rejecting non-image MIME types with error 'Unsupported file type. Accepted: jpeg, png, webp', limits.fileSize = 20 * 1024 * 1024

For each accepted file: generate uuid v4 as id, insert asset row with id, filename=originalname, filepath=uploads/<uuid>-<originalname>, mime_type, file_size=bytes, category='uploaded', enrichment_source='pending', created_at=now
Response 201: { uploaded: N, assets: [{ id, filename, filepath }] }
Route registered in server/src/index.js: app.use('/api/assets', uploadRouter) — note: upload route is POST /api/assets/upload so it must be registered before the :id param route
Supertest test: POST with a valid JPEG buffer returns 201 with an asset id
Supertest test: uploaded asset is retrievable via GET /api/assets/:id
Supertest test: file exists on disk at the filepath in the response
Negative case: POST with a .txt file returns 400 { error: 'Unsupported file type. Accepted: jpeg, png, webp' }
Negative case: POST with no files field returns 400 { error: 'No files provided' }
Negative case: multer fileSize error returns 413 { error: 'File too large. Maximum size is 20MB' }

stories.title: POST /api/assets/:id/enrich — OpenAI Vision enrichment endpoint
stories.description: As a content author, I want uploaded images enriched with AI-generated metadata so they are immediately descriptive and searchable.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/services/enrich.js exports enrichAsset(db, id, openaiClient)
enrichAsset reads asset row from DB by id, checks filepath is not null and file exists on disk
Reads image file as Buffer, converts to base64 string

Calls openaiClient.chat.completions.create with model: 'gpt-4o', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'image_url', image_url: { url: 'data:<mime>;base64,<data>' } }, { type: 'text', text: '<prompt>' }] }]

Prompt instructs model: 'Analyze this image and respond with ONLY valid JSON, no markdown: { "title": string, "description": string, "tags": string[], "location": string|null, "scene": string, "subjects": string[], "mood": string, "channel_hint": string, "confidence": number 0-1 }'

Parses JSON from response, updates assets row: enriched_title, enriched_description, enriched_tags=JSON.stringify(tags), enriched_location, enriched_scene, enriched_subjects=JSON.stringify(subjects), enriched_mood, enriched_channel=channel_hint, enrichment_source='ai-vision', enrichment_confidence, updated_at=now
POST /api/assets/:id/enrich route in server/src/routes/assets.js calls enrichAsset, returns 200 with updated full asset record
OpenAI client initialized in server/src/index.js only if OPENAI_API_KEY is set, passed to routes via app.locals.openai
If OPENAI_API_KEY not set: route returns 503 { error: 'AI enrichment unavailable — OPENAI_API_KEY not configured' }
Jest test (jest.mock openai): enrichAsset maps all response fields correctly to DB columns
Jest test (jest.mock openai): after enrich, asset has non-null enriched_title in DB
Jest test (jest.mock openai): after enrich, FTS search on enriched_title returns the asset
Negative case: asset has no filepath → 400 { error: 'No image file available for enrichment' }
Negative case: filepath not found on disk → 404 { error: 'Image file not found on disk' }
Negative case: OpenAI API throws → 502 { error: 'Enrichment failed', detail: message } — asset row unchanged
